# Privacy Policy

Privacy Policy

## Uk Gdpr

Introduction

Who we are

[Company Name] ("we", "us") provides [Product Name], a UK-only workspace platform for notes, work boards, teams, and search.

[Product Name] is offered exclusively in the United Kingdom (England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland). Customer data is processed and stored in Microsoft Azure UK South. [Company Name] does not offer multi-region tenancy, data residency selection, or services outside the UK.

[Product Name] is a workspace and productivity platform. It is not a medical device, clinical system, or regulated health record. Users must not rely on [Product Name] for diagnosis, treatment, or clinical decision-making. AI outputs are assistive drafts only.

This Privacy Policy explains how we collect and use personal data when you visit our website, create an account, or use [Product Name].

It is written for users in the United Kingdom, including England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland, and reflects UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018.

Data we collect

Account and identity

Name, email, organisation, and authentication identifiers via Azure AD B2C

Workspace preferences and notification settings

Billing and subscription metadata (business customers)

Content you provide

Notes, work items, comments, attachments, and team membership

Optional voice recordings for transcription (stored in Azure Blob, UK South)

Search queries and activity necessary to operate the service

Technical data

IP address, device/browser type, session logs, and security audit events

Aggregated usage telemetry (no prompt or raw AI response content in logs)

How we use data

Purposes and lawful bases

Purpose

Data

Lawful basis (UK GDPR)

Provide and secure the service

Account, content, logs

Contract; Legitimate interests (security)

AI-assisted features (summarise, transcribe, etc.)

Note/work content in-memory

Contract; Consent where required for optional features

Billing and support

Contact, subscription

Contract; Legal obligation

Improve reliability and abuse prevention

Telemetry metadata

Legitimate interests

Comply with law

Any relevant data

Legal obligation

AI features

Transparency

Where AI is used, we indicate this in the product. AI runs on Azure OpenAI in UK South. Prompts and raw model outputs are not stored; only outputs you choose to save are persisted.

You should review AI-generated text before relying on it. See our AI Transparency Statement.

Sharing and retention

Processors

We use UK-region subprocessors listed in our Subprocessor List (e.g. Microsoft Azure). We do not sell personal data.

Customer administrators may export workspace data subject to their organisation's policies.

Retention

We retain data according to our Data Retention Schedule and your organisation's settings. Backups are retained for disaster recovery then deleted per policy.

On account closure, production data is deleted within 90 days except where law requires longer retention.

Your rights

UK GDPR rights

You may request access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, or object to certain processing. Contact privacy@[company-domain].

You may complain to the ICO. If you use [Product Name] through an employer, your organisation may be the controller for workspace content — contact them first for content requests.
